mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above its 12-month trend, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This dual constraint produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during high-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons per month signals robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with PAYEMS levels (Spearman rho < 0.10), or returns are negative in > 50% of months when PAYEMS > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,200 thousand units signals weakening residential demand and forward-looking economic contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity markets reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,200 thousand units in month-over-month comparison
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances when HOUST < 1,200k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling supply-side weakness combined with labor market softening. This dual deterioration produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in prior month
regime_hint: post-expansion slowdown with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.8% month-over-month, indicating strong consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations. This produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with RSAFS growth rates (Spearman rho < 0.08), or returns are negative in > 52% of months with RSAFS growth > 0.8%.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5%, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending power. Equity markets reprice earnings expectations downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% in month-over-month comparison
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 62% of instances when UNRATE > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real interest rates (approximated by FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation rate) exceed 1.5%, monetary policy is restrictive in real terms. This suppresses equity valuations through elevated discount rates and reduced real asset returns, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
regime_hint: high real rates regime with restrictive monetary stance
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of triggered instances, or the real rate spread shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously contract month-over-month. This dual demand weakness signals recession risk that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity markets reprice terminal value downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND RSAFS month-over-month change < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1=23,386.733 billion and RSAFS=718,867 million
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.
